story_shape: the anchor of one of the nation's best pass-protecting lines -- an All-American guard back with his whole interior

plan.spine: a third-team All-American guard returns to anchor a Cincinnati interior that allowed the second-fewest sacks in the country
blend: 2025: third-team AP All-American; 85.4 PFF grade (3rd-rated guard)
Cincinnati's line allowed just 7 sacks -- 2nd nationally
returns with the interior trio intact
plan.payoff: third-team All-America and a line that barely allowed a sack
plan.forward_stake: whether the returning interior makes Cincinnati a Big 12 contender

hook: Cincinnati allowed the second-fewest sacks in the country, and Evan Tengesdahl was the anchor -- a third-team All-American guard who's back, with his whole interior alongside him.
expand: Tengesdahl's 2025 earned third-team AP All-America honors and an 85.4 PFF grade that ranked among the nation's top guards, the steadying force on a Cincinnati line that allowed just seven sacks all season -- second-fewest in the country. At 6-3, 320, he's already an ESPN Way-Too-Early first-team All-American for 2026, and crucially, the interior returns intact around him. The forward stakes are a Big 12 contender's foundation: lines this good don't usually come back whole, and an offense that protects this well gives Cincinnati a real ceiling. He's the kind of interior anchor a coordinator builds protection schemes around.
style: Tengesdahl is a high-grade interior pass protector with the size to handle power -- 6-3, 320, with an 85.4 PFF mark that placed him among the best guards in the country. The team-level proof is the seven sacks allowed (second nationally), a number you don't post without an interior that holds the pocket's middle. He's shown versatility across the line in spring, the mark of a lineman with the technique to play multiple spots. He's a steady, powerful, low-mistake guard -- the unglamorous interior anchor that elite pass protection is built on.
style_layers_pending: no PBP for an offensive lineman -- team sacks-allowed + PFF grade + All-America honors grounded; fan-vs-rival discourse descriptors pending discourse-extraction cleanup
